steps.title: Use the slope directly
steps.content: No model fitting is needed here because the problem gives the slope of the line of best fit: about $57$. In context, that means the model predicts $57$ additional beach visitors for each $1^\circ\text{C}$ increase.

steps.title: Scale the rate to 5 degrees
steps.content: A $5^\circ\text{C}$ increase is five one-degree increases, so multiply the slope by $5$: $57\times5=285$.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The question asks for the increase in predicted visitors, not the total number of visitors. The predicted increase is 285.
